reviews.review_title: Metal gear solid, but you’re a robot.
reviews.review_description: I’ve written a review before. But that was years ago. I just wanted to come back and say that this game is awesome and was one of my first vr games I had! I haven’t played it in a while and should probably redownload it at some point. Really hope that this gets a third installment at some point. You guys are the best, keep it up!

developer_response.body:
Hey there RollerPrime. We greatly appreciate your review. SO stoked you loved it when you tried, and hope the game can continue to entertain. It's our dream to continue the Espire franchise, and continue to support these existing Espire games too. All the best!
- EspireMike from LODE

reviews.review_description:
I bought this game long ago, years, today i installed it to find out that the best part of it is missing, the MR missions! And also found out that now you have to pay for it!! Developers, what the hell!?!? You downgrade a product just like that? Unacceptable.

Update: After being contacted by the developer I learned that the MR Missions package is free for owners of Espire 2. (If bought previous to the MR Individual launch), which I am. I appreciate the help and clarification. I have no problems at all to now award 5 stars to this really cool game.
Regards.

developer_response.body:
Hello. Don't worry. Anyone that owned Espire 2 before December 16, 2025 gets "Espire: MR Missions" for free. Espire: MR Missions was turned into its own game, but we spent months with Meta to ensure there would be a free entitlement for existing Espire 2 owners. That said, players have been having issues getting it, which unfortunately we can only do our best to help resolve within our capacity. Send an email to contact@espirevr.com - or feel free to contact us via DM'ing our social accounts. We will then make sure you get access to the MR game. 

Cheers, 
EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire.

reviews.review_title: Quite interesting but a bit repetitive
reviews.review_description:
First of all, thanks to the YouTube channel @vraimentcoolofficiel and their contest for giving me the opportunity to play this game by winning a key.
It is a nice stealth game between Hitman and avatar-robocop (as you get to control a robot). However, it is quite demanding as it's hard to get all the stars and you need a good strategy. Missions are long and there are checkpoints but I wished I could save more often. 
You get to control different units but there are no big differences. Movement and actions are not very optimized, physics and immersion are in a very basic level. The weapon system is particular (need energy to unblock the weapons found and the bullets are not sufficient) but it's more focused on infiltration.
All in all, it's a nice game for the fans of this gender and I recommend giving it a try.

reviews.review_title: The best game on oculus but one thing…
reviews.review_description:
Ok the one thing is not your fault it’s that no nobody plays multiplayer!!!!!!! But besides that best game on oculus here’s my review 👇

Graphics 8/10 they are ok but some things are blurry.

Guns 9/10 some times a little glitchy.

Story 9/10 it actually makes sense.

Multiplayer 9/10 fun with friends 

Single player 8/10 solid but I like multiplayer games more sorry. 😞

Music 100000000000000000/10 soooooooooooooo good!!

Sinder 9/10 can be annoying if you can’t get in a certain vent.

Sooty 8/10 could be better his guns don’t fit his hand make him bigger too.


That’s my review bye!!!!!!!! P.S. Will there be an espire 3?

developer_response.body:
Hey MaydayVR, thanks for your incredible review. So stoked you have been enjoying the game. I'm the Director of the project and I agree with you about the music. It's my favourite videogame soundtrack hehe. I am just a little bit biased.

Re the multiplayer, yes, it can be hard to find players through matchmaking. We invite players to check out our official Discord server @ https://discord.gg/EspireVR to team up with others. We've also added a "Lone Wolf" Mode to play the co-op campaign in singleplayer. 

Hope Espire 2 continues to entertain. As for Espire 3. It's certainly a dream. Hopefully one day. 
- EspireMike from LODE (Developers of Espire)

reviews.review_description:
You take a game , you iron out any kinks, you make a sequel and improve the game in every way possible,
Brilliant story, superb gameplay with stealth  being once again your best friend, but like I usually do, I go in guns blazing.
This looks amazing on my Quest 3 and with the impending MR Missions being added for free this has to be an essential buy this Christmas.
The action is just on point with the game, I like how you are able to use voice and touch commands.
Another amazing game to add to your collection.
Bravo Lode.

Honestly, I found the first chapter better. This one is more linear, and the addition of Sooty is almost useless since the choice isn't optional because you are forced to use him at certain points. The environment should allow you to choose the avatar you prefer, obviously changing your strategy. Voice commands don't work, you get caught out of nowhere for no plausible reason, the enemies are dumber than in the first chapter, no notable power has been added, it's perhaps shorter as well. In terms of personal taste and as an artist, Sinder's 3D model was better in the first chapter. Between overly positive reviews and too much curiosity, I couldn't wait and I paid full price, whereas now it's on sale and perhaps is worth what it really costs. But I didn't know that.

reviews.review_title: Espire MR still showing I have to buy it
reviews.review_description: I've owned espire 2 since launch and I accidentally bought Mr missions thinking it was something new then I learn I'm supposed to have received it for free since I own espire 2 well how TF did I just get tricked into buying it just now? Terrible ethics, this is what happens when you support small studios

developer_response.body:
Hi. We're sorry you're experiencing an issue getting a free copy of Espire: MR Missions. We spent a long time trying to get this set up with Meta and unfortunately it is just not working correctly for everyone. We are doing all we can to ensure you or other players that owned Espire 2 before DEC 16 2025 get a free copy of Espire MR. At this point, i'm manually providing keys to any players affected. 

I recommend that you request a refund for Espire: MR Missions from the Meta support team. Then, contact us via 'contact@espirevr.com' and I will ensure you get your free copy. 

We hope your issue is resolved. if so, we'd appreciate you revise the review for Espire 2 here as this is sadly a Meta store issue and it doesn't really have anything to do with Epsire 2 the game. 

Regards, 
EspireMike from LODE (Developers of Espire).

reviews.review_description:
Espire 2 is a sequel that builds brilliantly on the original, offering smoother gameplay, smarter AI, and a fantastic mix of stealth and action. The missions are varied and immersive, with environments that feel alive and full of opportunities for creative infiltration with much better graphics.

Switching between different Espire frames gives a tactical layer and keeps every mission feeling fresh. Controls are tight, movement feels natural, and the voice commands and gadget systems make you feel like a true covert operative.

The campaign is fun from start to finish, with an easy building tutorial. Whether you’re sneaking through ventilation shafts or going loud with gadgets blazing.  This can be played seated or standing and has several comfort modes, for me, I do wish it had Teleportion Mode though to help with my motion sickness.

A must-buy for stealth fans and VR players looking for a polished, exciting experience.
I miss the Mr mission's :( (btwi owned the game BEFORE th update)

developer_response.body:
Hey there! If you owned Espire 2 before Dec 16 2025, you'll get Espire: MR for free. If you're not seeing Espire MR for free, send me an email via contact@espirevr.com and i'll be sure you get a copy. We have had a mechanism in place with the Meta store for existing Espire 2 owners, but it has not been working as planned. 

Also, we've set up a 'release channel' for Espire 2 that is named "Legacy Version - with Espire Mixed Reality". Players can switch over to that and the Meta store will download the old version of Espire 2 with the MR Mode intact, if desired. It's possible to switch to that legacy version and back to the latest version whenever you want. We just dont support the legacy MR Mode anymore. 

Hope that helps, please do reach out via email if you haven't received your free copy. 

Cheers, 
EspireMike from LODE.

reviews.review_description:
Bonus! Get the new MR version free when you purchase this version 🤟 

Espire 2: Stealth Operatives VR is a brilliant evolution of VR stealth, delivering a slick, confident experience that feels purpose-built for virtual reality. From the moment you drop into your Espire unit, the game makes you feel like a high-tech covert operative rather than a player fumbling with VR controls.

The stealth gameplay is the real star here. Espire 2 gives you freedom: crawl through vents, lean around corners, silently take down enemies, or go loud when things inevitably go wrong. The AI is noticeably improved, reacting convincingly to sound and movement, which keeps every encounter tense and rewarding. It strikes a great balance between tactical planning and fast, reactive decision-making.

Movement and comfort are exceptionally well handled. Whether you prefer smooth locomotion, teleportation, or a mix of both, Espire 2 accommodates all playstyles, making it accessible without sacrificing immersion. The physical interactions grabbing ledges, reloading weapons, and using gadgets feel natural and responsive, reinforcing the sense that you’re truly inside the mission.

Visually, Espire 2 looks sharp and clean on standalone VR, with detailed environments and strong lighting that enhance stealth gameplay. Levels are varied and thoughtfully designed, encouraging experimentation and multiple approaches rather than a single “correct” path.

One of Espire 2’s standout additions is the co-op mode. Working with a friend to coordinate stealth takedowns, split objectives, and cover each other adds a whole new layer of fun and replayability. It’s not just a bonus feature it genuinely changes how the game feels and plays.

Overall, Espire 2: Stealth Operatives VR is a polished, satisfying, and highly replayable stealth experience. With smart level design, excellent comfort options, and engaging co-op, it stands as one of the strongest VR stealth games available and a must-play for fans of tactical action in virtual reality. 🎮🥽

UPDATE LONE WOLF
DEAR DEV THAnk YOU SO MUCH.
PLEASE IF THERE IS ADDITIONAL LEVEL DLC….WOULD PAY FOR IT

CHANGE YOUR LEVEL CONCEPTION FOR SOLO CAMPAIGN 
PLAYING SOLO WHAT WAS INTENDED FOR MULTIPLAYER WAS MORE FUN THAN SOLO CAMPAIGN BECAUSE LEVel were MORE OPEN..COULD EASILY CHOOSE DIFFETENT PATH HENCE BETTER REPLAYABOLITY…YOUR GAME HAS THE   THE BEST AI OF ALL GAMES …THANK YOU

developer_response.body:
Hey deathoncredit. Thanks for your review - we really appreciate it. We're making note of your feedback and will certainly take it onboard for any Espire 2 future content.

 We see what you mean regarding the Co-op campaign - it is more open-ended compared to the Singleplayer campaign. We could suggest that you try Espire 1: VR Operative if you've not tried it before. That's our other title and the missions in Espire 1 are similar to the Espire 2 co-op campaign. 

Thanks again for playing and we hope you continue to enjoy the game.

Cheers, 
EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire

reviews.review_description:
This game does what a sequel to a game should do, like in decades past: a clear progression from their first project with many lessons obviously learned. The developers deserved more success.

It's a well-crafted stealth game with a solid narrative, and ofc it's always fresh to have fiction set in my own country instead of The Empire.

I like to join public matches but I never waited so long for one public match honestly I hate that so can guys plssssss make public matches. One more  Question: IF YOUR NOT GOING TO ADD PUBLIC MATCHES WHY WOULD WE WHAIT FOR ONE!?

developer_response.body:
Hey there. Thanks for leaving your review! We do have public matches in the game however yes, it can be hard to find a co-op partner through the public matchmaking sometimes. You could join our Discord @ https://discord.gg/EspireVR and match up with other Espire players there. We've also recently added "Lone Wolf mode" where you can play Espire 2's co-op campaign in singleplayer mode. We added that mode because, yes, it can be hard to match up with another player sometimes, and the Espire 2 co-op campaign is a unique campaign that some players have never tried before. 

Cheers,

EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire

reviews.review_description: Ngl, probably the best stealth game of all time, i could go in depth, but it’s better if you play it yourself. Limited in content though. Also the mark ones are scary￼

reviews.review_title: Enjoying it but a bit buggy
reviews.review_description:
I got this is part of the Meta horizon’s deal that came with my new quest 3.  Overall, I am enjoying it, but it does seem a bit buggy…. I’m on level three and all of a sudden it seems like I’m super short - I’ve reset my guardian and manually reset the floor, but literally all of a sudden I’m like below/shorter than the desks in an office. I’ll try everything to fix it but there’s a lot of stuff I just can’t reach.  

It does also just crash from time to time, but I’m finding it is saving my progress no problem

developer_response.body:
Hello. Thanks for playing Espire 2! Glad you've enjoyed it overall. Regarding feeling short - it's actually a feature of the game. You've reached the point in the campaign where you can command the "Sooty" frame. It's a 3FT tall Espire frame perfect for infiltration, with a deployable 'noisemaker' device built into its hands. 

We added Sooty to our game because in VR, we had this magic opportunity to make players 'feel' 6FT or 3FT tall by artificially scaling their 'interpapillary distance' and world scale...you can't do that in traditional games. 

If f you don't like Sooty, then after this short moment in the campaign where you are forced to be Sooty, you can optionally choose between the taller Sinder or Sooty frame going forwards. 

Note:  the Mixed Reality game mode doesn't have Sinder or Sooty frames - your height will just be whatever your actual height is in real life. 

Cheers. 

- Mike from LODE

reviews.review_description: Can you make a dirt one? I don’t care if you run a marathon with this game Metal gear solid if you ever played the game with snake this game reminds you just like it absolutely awesome absolutely amazing I thought the first one was great. The second one is better. And I hope they make a third one. The development is a genius. Thank you for making a game of so enjoyable.

developer_response.body:
Thankyou for playing our games, and for taking the time to leave a review, it really helps out. We loved reading this and are so stoked you've enjoyed the 2 Espire games. We're HUGE MGS fans, and it has been a dream opportunity to be able to make our own Stealth Action games. 

Our dream is to make a 3rd. Maybe one day! 

Cheers, 

Mike from LODE

reviews.review_title: PEAK, but wish people still played
reviews.review_description: Game is great, but I haven't been able to play multiplayer in a while.

developer_response.body: We're glad you like Espire 2 and we really appreciate your review. Re Multiplayer, we're working on a new Espire 2 update which adds 'lone wolf' mode. This is a single-player version of our co-op mode. We also are @ discord.gg/EspireVR where you can team up with other Espire agents and play Co Op. Cheers!

reviews.review_title: Great game mind blowing only1 problem
reviews.review_description: I only jumped into this game today so far I absolutely  love it ! absolutely amazing and well thought out game the only problem I have come across this far that is impairing ability to play is mission 3 just after you change your suit you for some odd reason become extremely short and it definitely doesn't seem like it's actually ment to be that way .I tried to recalibrate the waist and chest and it hasn't helped I also tried adjusting my height and no luck  it makes you feel as if you are only three feet tall . I'm hoping to get past 3 and it changes and hopefully it doesn't happen in MR as I specifically got this to play the mixed reality part .

developer_response.body:
Hey there! So glad you've been enjoying the game overall. Actually, you've reached the point in the campaign where you can command the "Sooty" frame. It is indeed a 3FT tall Espire frame perfect for infiltration, with a deployable 'noisemaker' device built into its hands. 

We added Sooty to our game because in VR, we had this magic opportunity to make players 'feel' 6FT or 3FT tall by artificially scaling their 'interpapillary distance' and world scale...you can't do that in traditional games. But we understand if you don't like Sooty! This point in the campaign, you're required to use Sooty, but afterwards you can optionally choose between the taller Sinder or Sooty frame going forwards. 

The Mixed Reality game mode doesn't have Sinder or Sooty frames - your height will just be whatever your actual height is in real life. 

Hope you enjoy the game otherwise. Thanks for playing!

Mike from LODE

reviews.review_description: I’ve had Espire 2 for a while now but I just got around to trying it out. I’m not very far in but it is definitely off to an excellent start. I can’t say much for the story yet but it is a true singleplayer stealth campaign. You are rewarded for playing in stealth. You can go in guns blazing if you choose to or if you’re detected and SHTF you can shoot your way out but it is so much more rewarding to sneak through areas without killing anyone or being detected. I love how you can climb,go through ventilation ducts as well as disabling cameras,laser detectors and gun turrets. I love being able to sneak up on enemies knock them out,tase them or put them to sleep with a dart. You can then hide their bodies to prevent to prevent alerting other enemies on patrol. So far,the only issue I’ve had was not being able to insert a new magazine in the pistol. This game is excellent if you’re like me and prefer single player story driven campaigns. I’m still early in the game and as love it so far. If I run into any issues as I progress further into the game,I will definitely come back and update my review. As of right now,this game is a breath of fresh air. The developers have done a phenomenal job on this game

reviews.review_title: Great concept, but rough around the edge
reviews.review_description:
This is a good game with the clever mechanic of being able to climb around the map. 
-Compared to the original, it offers more diverse environments and an easier way to lure enemies with voice devices/talking. 
-The repair device and tranquilizer being built into the wrist is also a good improvement. 
-Repair device energy is somewhat finite, which is a good balance. Still, I really didn’t need felt like I was going to run out.
-Weapon controls were also made a bit more realistic. You can now actually change magazines. 
-Weapons seem to have a bit more variety in stats, but I rarely felt I needed a specific weapon.
-They also added a different robot unit for a bit more variety.

However, I still think there are some areas where the game falls flat.
- For starters, they removed the adjustable belt/holsters, which was a nice accessibility feature to have. 
-Holster hitboxes are still kind of clunky. I would end up grabbing something I didn’t want because how closer everything is. I think a solution would be to either have a back holster for a weapon or the ammo (like Half Life). 
-Hitboxes on the weapons themselves are also weird, and I would end up grabbing a magazine when trying to grab the front handle. Pumping the shotgun was also VERY finicky.
-Having a new robot is nice, but it almost felt useless because on some missions it would force you to switch to the first type. Also, having the wrist weapon slot be fixed to the type of robot kind of discouraged my use of the second type. I like the concept, I just think it was underutilized and somewhat unnecessary when considering the other buffs.

Overall, about a 7/10 game. Fun to play through once or twice and then go to the bonus content (the Augmented Reality mode was cool). The formula just needs a bit more refinement. It’s just a short, fun and easy experience all the way through.

Woah!!
How did they make it even better than the first?!

Somehow they have improved the graphics, sound and even game! 😮

It’s more of the same as the first game.. you don’t need to have played the first, to play this.

This one brings multiplayer, MR and weekly tasks to the game… aswell as the usual single player story mode.

Graphics are some of the best on a Quest game.

On the first game, where you could play stealthily or all guns blazing (I played this way), this second instalment favours stealth game play.

Again it has the choice of using your own voice to shout out to the enemy.
You have more gadgets to use, built in repair, tranquilliser gun ect… even the night vision which is a cool design on doing that.

Animations are nice and smooth, so is the game play.
Like the first game, it reminds me of a VR Metal Gear Solid game. .. which is a great thing of course!

I highly recommend this game if you like stealth gameplay, fps.. or a fan the MGS games.

Great job devs!!

reviews.review_title: Impressed - until it wiped my progress
reviews.review_description: UPDATE: Game crashed on startup and wiped 8 hours of progress. It idid this before when I had just started. Once can be forgiven but twice costs it 2 stars on the review. I am not going to start a 3rd time - uninstalled. ORIGINAL: It's a full fat stealth game on Quest. I have not looked at it since launch. I thought it was just gimmicky with the MR modes but I got it on Horizon plus and the game loop is great. Real fun. Big missions that you can take your time doing. Big surprise. Just a solid game

developer_response.body:
We're extremely sorry to hear that you lost progress. That's a #1 priority issue we'd like to address. We're not aware of any current Savegame issues that are due to the game itself. We've seen some issues with Meta Cloud Sync before. Please note that our game stores a rotating backup of your last 3 session's save games. They're stored on your Quest Headset so you'll need access to a PC or a 3rd party app to access them. These backup saves are included in the Meta Cloud Backup Routine alongside the main save. We will look at adding an in-game menu to swap between backup saves in a situation like this. Again, sincere apologies you've experienced a save game issue like this. 

Regards, 
Mike from Digital Lode.

reviews.review_description: so when i played it it has not been a while since i've played it i could not get past the tutorial because of the time

Played for a bit and got to mission 2 the whistleblower and it wouldn't let me progress about half way through. The controls were pretty iffy and no jump button so when climbing I was throwing myself into oblivion until I could get onto a platform. Might go back to it but I dunno.

developer_response.body: Thanks for trying out the game so far. Sorry to hear you're stuck in mission 2. We're happy to help here or on our Discord. **Spoiler** Half way through Mission 2, you'll need to locate a SINDER frame. The Sooty frame can't proceed. Eliminate a guard and drag the guard over to the retinal scanner. This will gain access to the whistleblower's suite. Hack the laptop and use your repair tool to download the data from the Yellow orb that floats above the laptop.

reviews.review_description: I love this game, the reviews are a bit misleading because I haven’t ran into a single bug or problem in this game except for the fact that I may not want to get off of the game. 10/10.
There's much which could be improved over the original but overall it's a fun game having stealth elements
it sucks do not play

I was enjoying the game, I just started playing it, but I brought it ages after I got espire and enjoyed it.. ..HOWEVER MISSION 4 just keeps booting me out to oculus home screen 😭 I tried uninstall and reinstall, looks like I'll not get to play past this mission 😩 if anyone can help please

the game looks fantastic and you can see a lot of work has gone into it. I love the heavy feeling of the movement. 

couldn't really progress through the opening section, it really wasn't overly clear what was happening, was also very long and couldn't skip through the dialogue. 

I got stuck first trying pull a lever, I tried for ages but it would not grab and the camera was going crazy. 

Got stuck the second time when I needed to grab a gun off the floor from an enemy. movement was frozen and I was told to grab the weapon from the floor, tried for ages and the weapon would not come towards me. 

I would really like to play the game but I don't want to go through the whole opening section again.

reviews.review_title: Is a good game but too short
reviews.review_description: Is a good and entertaining game but way too short ,no extra content ,if you want to play single you finish the game in 1 hour

developer_response.body: We're glad you tried out Espire 2 and we must say, how on earth did you finish this in 1 hour? There's a Mixed Reality game mode with approx 1-2 hours of content. A VR singleplayer campaign that's around 5-6 hours of content, an Espire Ops 'side mission' mode with 12 missions - that's another 1-2 hours at least. Finally a Co-Op VR Campaign which is around 2 hours long. If you can complete the whole thing within 1 hour, would you like to work as a QA Tester? We'd love to be able to run whole game tests that quickly.

reviews.review_description: Hidden gem, my favorite vr game of all time. The depth to the actions is incredible and experiences like this are what I use vr for
Great stealth gameplay, excellent player freedom. It’s like playing Hitman in VR, except I’ve played Hitman in VR and this is better.

developer_response.body: Thankyou sincerely for your review. We can't state just how much a review like this means to us. We poured our hearts and souls into Espire 2. We're a small team and I think the budget for our entire game would be a fraction of the larger AAA stealth titles. We're also incredibly stoked to be among such awesome stealth action games in VR. Still dreaming that Konami make an MGS VR game...one day.

reviews.review_description: I can t  delete it like wtf

developer_response.body:
We're sorry to hear you can't delete the game. It's unfortunately something we have Zero control over. That's an OS-level function. We recommend you open a support ticket with Meta and hope it resolves the issue for you.

Cheers, Mike from LODE.

reviews.review_description: Seems like it could be a cool game, but there's a lot of fluff mechanics in the game that could have been 86ed. The worst thing about this game is the devs decided to put reverse turn mechanics into it. It's just outdated into oblivion. You can turn your body for smooth motion, sure, but there's times it's better to use the joystick. And in this game and others that use this mechanic, the world goes the opposite way, instead of, ya know, staying still. Which is a 100% uninstall from me and the wife. I play some nauseating games with fall and jump mechanics, but the reverse turning world is a travesty.

developer_response.body:
Hi there. We're unsure what you mean about reverse turning. The game offers snap turning or smooth turning. By default, Snap/Smooth turn is on the right thumbstick and there's an option to swap sticks if you would prefer it on the left thumbstick. Snap/Smooth turn towards the right and your perspective snaps/turns right. 

For players that experience simulator sickness there's also a unique comfort mode known as the "Espire Control Theater" found in the 'Comfort' settings. It will render the 'control theater' environment that POE stands in within your peripheral vision. At it's most extreme setting, it should eliminate simulator sickness.

reviews.review_title: So much fun sneaking past guards
reviews.review_description:
This game is so much fun.

Get to be a sneaky spy and climb things and shoot a dart gun or a gun and it's just so much fun, but it's definitely story based rather than just a killing game.

developer_response.body: Hi there. Thanks for taking the time to leave not only a rating, but a review. We're glad to read you've been having fun with Espire 2! Cheers from LODE - Espire Devs.

reviews.review_description: I’ve had espire 2 for a little over a year maybe 2 now and never really played single player campaign but I played the first level of single player and realized this game is amazing and might be one of if not the best stealth vr games of all time I’d love to see an espire 3 and the devs need to keep up this amazing work 👍🏽

This game is fully immersive & a gem to play. If you like stealth games w/ unique play mechanics you’re in for a treat. This is a love letter to games like Splinter Cell. 
A few things, works good sitting, works better standing. Turn off motion sickness settings for smoother gameplay. Let yourself get sucked in by playing on story before attempting a higher difficulty. The mechanics are a lot at 1st but once you get the hang of it, you’ll be taking out cameras, darting armed guards, crawling through vents, and saving the day!

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to leave a review, and we are stoked to hear you've been enjoying Espire 2. We're massive Stealth Action fans. We've worn our inspiration on our sleeves and have relished the opportunity to make Espire 1 & 2. We hope to see more stealth action games in VR!

reviews.review_description: Incredible game had LOADS of fun with the mixed reality! There is no other game for Quest that comes CLOSE!

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review of Espire's MR mode. We had a blast developing it and it's great to hear you've been enjoying it. Cheers from LODE - Espire Devs.

reviews.review_title: Haven’t tried MR yet or multiplayer but.
reviews.review_description: THIS IS AWESOME! TRANQUILIZERS? SURRENDERING ENEMIES WITH YIUR ACTUAL VOICE?! WAY TOO GOOD.
If you are bored and want to play a game that requires skills and stealth then this game if for you I just finished playing and I freaking LOVED it 10/10 definitely recommend.
exactly as advertised!

Still rating good because the presentation of the game is gorgeous in the store but I downloaded yesterday and the game won’t open for me and my roommate who recently got the game as well the loading screen just never goes away and then the game crashes and takes 2-4 retries to get back to just the loading screen. Just wondering what’s happening.

developer_response.body:
Hi there! We are sorry to hear you're encountering this issue. Just to confirm, does Espire 2 ever get to launch correctly? The very first time you play, music will play and there is a one-time loading sequence that takes 2 minutes. There should be a progress bar and countdown timer. It only occurs the first time you play the game. 

If you're not getting to the point where music is playing / Espire loading bar, then the game may not have installed correctly. Developers have been made aware of a recent bug with the latest Quest OS version where sometimes games 4+GB in size will not install correctly. If it's an installation issue, it's unfortunately out of our control to help. We'd suggest contacting Meta Quest support. Please update this review if your issue isn't solved, we can suggest further workarounds. Sorry to hear you've not been able to play the game!

reviews.review_description: I got to the 2nd or 3rd auto save and it made my headset freeze for hours and I couldn’t get it to unfreeze
Weird... Just weird... It has some cool ideas but AC Nexus is a better stealth game.
Just this

reviews.review_title: Can’t handle large rooms and no MR coop
reviews.review_description: Disappointing. Can’t handle large rooms. And coop mode can’t be in mixed reality (such as spatial ops)

developer_response.body:
Hi Sayuna, thank you for the feedback. We appreciate you sharing your thoughts, and we are sorry the Mixed Reality setup bugs (specifically the room setup and boundary issues) ruined the experience for you.

We know your review was left a while ago. We are updating our response to let you know that we've removed the MR Missions mode from Espire 2 entirely. This allows Espire 2 to focus 100% on the VR stealth gameplay.

However, you still own that MR content! We have moved it to a standalone app called "Espire: MR Missions." All players that owned Espire 2 before Dec 16 2025 can claim a copy for free by visiting the new game's page on the Meta Quest Store. In that new app, we've addressed the room setup process and improved the way missions are initialized.

Since the broken MR integration is no longer in this app, would you be open to updating your review of Espire 2 to reflect the current state of the game? We would really appreciate it!

Cheers, 

EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire.

reviews.review_description: Good concept, though.

I went into this game blind and wasn’t sure what to expect aside from seeing a few clips online of folks playing it and sneaking up enemies. Needless to say, I was quite impressed with it. Like everyone has stated, this is a stealth based game but like in any stealth game, you’re not entirely required to be stealthy if you want to go in guns blazing into each mission. Though you’re rewarded better if you manage to get through without drawing fire or getting caught. The cool thing is that the levels are set up in a way that you can complete them in many different ways. I felt the game was solid, definitely high quality, interesting James Bond like storyline, great graphics, environments, and sound/music. Though I do think the AI could use some work as there were a few times I was literally right next to an enemy and he was basically oblivious to where I was. Other times they almost were able to see me right through the walls. But other than that I really enjoyed the game overall, and that’s just the campaign. Haven’t even tried the mix reality stuff yet but look forward to.

this game is basically an upgrade to the first one in every way Its probably the closest were going to get to metal gear in vr unless metal gear delta gets a VR mod but still if the dev's see this great game I hope you end up making Espire 3!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to leave a review for the game and for trying both Espire 1 & 2. We're so stoked you've been enjoying E2. We're massive MGS fans...that series was a huge inspiration for us. Fingers crossed we get to see MGS in VR in the future. We also have toes crossed we get to make "Espire 3" one day :) 

Cheers - LODE, Espire Devs.

reviews.review_title: Over a year later since I made a review
reviews.review_description: Love this game no complaints good job developers I highly recommend 😃😃😃👍👍👍

developer_response.body: Thanks for your support of our game and for coming back to review! We're so glad you love Espire 2! Hope the game continues to entertain. Cheers from LODE - Espire Devs.

reviews.review_description:
The single player campaign is fun so far. In this game stealth is optional but is recommended, so if you plan on being a deadly assassin in this game, you should probably reconsider. Stealth feels really nice and makes you feel like a real spy. This game is definitely worth the money. My only complaint is that the multiplayer mode is dead and the only people that join me are primitive apes that haven’t played the tutorial and aren’t on voice chat.

Update: the campaign is still really good, but it gets way too hard to sneak around after the first few levels. One of my main problems is how dying works. Rather than resetting everything, it just sends you back to the nearest checkpoint, which can lead to enemies being stuck in doorways and other annoyances. My other complaint is the end of mission briefing, it makes it where you have to not be risky and not try things out. I also wish killing enemies weren’t discouraged and instead being spotted and damaged enemies gets you a lower rank. Last thing is that instead of just respawning you should go back to your last save so any of the damage that you received to you and your rank.

The game has a lot of cool mechanics, and easter eggs

The mixed reality missions are dope

It has a ton of content, totally worth it
Even better than espire 1.  So many twist and turns.

developer_response.body:
Hello! Thanks so much for taking the time to leave a review of Espire 2, and for your support of our games + company. We are so glad you've enjoyed the sequel. We only had the opportunity to make Espire 2 because many fans supported us buying Espire 1. It was a dream opportunity for us to continue the story! Thanks again for your review. Cheers, 

- EspireMike from LODE, Espire devs.

reviews.review_description: Its a good game but i can’t even past the first level because i have stickdrift on my right hand controller most games have an option to turn off snap / smooth turn but this doesn’t so i’ve just payed for a game I can’t play please add this option to the settings.

developer_response.body:
Hi there - we're sorry to hear that you're caught out by stick drift on the right controller. We'd like to let you know we've added the ability to disable snap/smooth turns in our December 2024 update. We hope you can enjoy the game. 

Cheers, 

Mike from LODE

reviews.review_description: Poor graphics,clunky mechanics, poor sound and just plain boring. I wish I never bought this title. When playing i could not believe how bad the visuals and graphics were. Like frim the 90s. Don't waste your money like I did on this pos.  And it's a 4 hour game!
The game does not save progress

developer_response.body:
Hello. We're sorry to hear you've had an issue with the save game! We are not aware of any save game bugs with Espire 2. If you would like to provide us with more information, we'd love to help you. You can email us direct via support@espirevr.com or update your review here - we will get a notification.

Regarding the save feature, any time you update the system settings, the game will save. The VR Campaign will only save progress at 3-4 checkpoints throughout each mission. You'll know this has happened as the game will say 'mission progress saved'. 

The Espire Ops will save progress every time you complete an Esprie Op. We'd love to hear more about the issue you're facing and hope we can help resolve it!

Cheers, LODE - developers of Espire.

reviews.review_title: Stealth action at your house 25 % off
reviews.review_description:
I bought Espire 2 for the mixed reality experience. If avoiding lasers, sneaking up on enemies, and locking them up in closets in your living room sounds fun to you, this is the right game.

M Q 3 . B I O . L I N K (Just copy or type without the spaces)

One of the best MR games yet. There is a way to enable playing your whole flat or maybe even house by enabling developer mode on your Quest 3 and disabling the guardian. I still have to try it, but I will definitely check it out. 

If this was in any way helpful to you, please give it a 👍 up.

Gameplay and graphics is much better than Espire 1. Although there is still room for improvements regading graphics if you compare it to other games like Hubris. And it offers really too less content, the single player campaign is too short.
First off, amazing series. Also, brain damaged guy puppeting a robot going on adventures while having horse tranquilizers, and stun sticks for hands

This game is awesome. This will bring out a lot of nostalgia of Metal Gear Solid. The mechanics are awesome. The voice system is amazing. This is a must try.

developer_response.body:
Hi again, we're excited to update our response and let you know we just dropped our 1.3 update, which includes public matchmaking for our co-op campaign. We hope you enjoy matchmaking with another stealth operative around the globe to experience the co-op missions! If you do have fun, we'd appreciate if you'd consider updating your review with your thoughts!

Re difficulty, we'd suggest to check you're playing on the Normal difficulty, not 'story'. A hardcore difficulty option has been another request from players and is part of our 2023 roadmap. 

Hope you'll find great fun in Espire 2! Cheers.

reviews.review_title: Play the original not this garbage
reviews.review_description:
It’s not much of a sequel, for starters it doesn’t take off when the original ended, it barely has the same premise, and it changed almost everything just for the sake of change. For some reason the graphics on this one look a million times worse than the original, which I don’t even get. I remember the graphics on the quest 1 and somehow the graphics on the quest 2 are worse. The entire game just seems to have a much lower budget, the menu is worse, like a lot worse, and the effects in the game cease to exist. I wasn’t expecting a completely polished half life Alyx experience, but like there’s not even an effect like a spark for when you shoot a power box, how hard is it? I found it cool that the tranquilizer gun from the original was integrated into you, but it no longer had quality sound design, seriously the sound design is majorly lacking, and no more quality visual effects, like I couldn’t even see a bullet come out of it or an effect when it hits. The only reason to buy this over the original is for the multiplayer, other than that this is basically a cheap knockoff version of the original and it really shouldn’t be the same price.

Update: after playing some more it only got worse. The enemy AI is laughable compared to the originals, I went in front of it and it didn’t spot me since I was crouching. They seem to really, REALLY want to force the stealth part on you. In the first game you could either be all stealthy or go in guns blazing, in this game you need to use stealth because you can’t hold more than two magazines, it takes at least a full minute to unlock a weapon, so what’s even the point of having weapons. Also they removed the sticky cameras that you could throw and use to peek around corners which were one of my favorite features of the original, instead you have a normal camera that can only peak around corners and nothing else, you used to be able to use the sticky cameras to do things like lure guards with sound, not anymore. Also it only takes like what, 2 bullets to kill you? One guard shot me a couple times and I was dead. Besides all that the level design is just trash compared to the original, it feels so rushed. Also the graphics in the next few levels out of the tutorial got even worse somehow, how the hell was this made in unreal? You guys used unreal, to make this? I genuinely don’t know how you can mess up this badly, the graphics are so ugly it’s insane. I’m generally not picky about graphics, but there’s no style in this game, no consistency, and nothing at all about the graphics that makes me think it’s acceptable. If you really have to tone down the graphics THIS much to get a performant game, you probably haven’t optimized anything else. I really tried to give this game a second chance but it’s just not enjoyable. They took everything that made the first game good and removed it. Graphics are unbelievably worse, enemy AI seems like it was made in 1 day and had no testing, speaking of no testing, the settings menu doesn’t even work. I change a setting, it never updates, HOW do you mess up something this basic, did you play your own game at all? This is the biggest cash grab I’ve ever seen on the oculus store, there is nothing that makes this better than the original, there’s not even extra challenges anymore from what I can tell, so no replayability. They also treat you like a baby in this game, with every single thing being explained in the most boring way possible. Buy the first game not this, and if you’re an avid fan of the first game already, still don’t buy this. You will be disappointed.

developer_response.body: AVeryDankDuck, thanks for taking the time to leave a detailed review and we appreciate the feedback. To respond to some of your feedback. Story wise, the game does take off after the original ended. The co op campaign is set 2 years after Espire 1, and the single player campaign follows shortly after. We went to great lengths to try and get the original voice actors to reprise their roles, and the player assumes the role of the same character. Visually, we tried to be more ambitious with Espire 2 - with every level set in a different location, and including outdoor scenes as much as we could. Espire 1 was set inside 1 underground base. Soundwise, we added a lot of ambient effects, when Espire 1 didnt have any ambience. Appreciate the feedback about the missing spark particle when shooting a power box, that is something we can look at for our post-launch update. We already are working on Update 1.1 which includes visual polish and some audio balancing. Regards, Digital Lode.

reviews.review_description: I so wanted to enjoy this game as I loved the first one so much, unfortunately there is a bug that won't let you past the initial tutorial about 70% of the time. It's when you have to sneak up on the guy and tell him to drop his weapon, you are (most of the time) not able to pick it up and have to restart until it works, I hate buggy stuff like this and don't understand why you guys cannot properly QA your products.

developer_response.body:
Hi gary.bryan.37, we're really sorry to hear you've encountered this issue. We want to investigate it and hopefully find a solution so you don't have to keep restarting the level. We'd love it if you could help us by providing some more information by either updating this review or joining our discord: https://discord.gg/espirevr

can you let us know the following:
-were you using your voice or the voice commands menu?
-can you try repeating the command if you are unable to pickup the gun, and see if that lets you pick it up on 2nd try
-if you used the command buttons, did you press the button once or multiple times?

reviews.review_description:
No complaints on the game as it is, though I want to exhort that I bought it to play multi-player online, and I still see no logic in finding a friend to share a room code before I even found someone to play this game with to actually become friends and know they have this game before finding one.

Also, the Facebook has no dedicated page to find a friend that would like to cooperate gameplay to eventually share room codes with.

Could there be made a private game for the room code sharing, but also, a public join and a public host so from that point on can be used to find a friend for private games if the need arises?

Most games have made this optional difference, and it's very good to do so. Regards.

developer_response.body:
Hello ZenMushin! We wanted to update our developer response to let you know: We just dropped our 1.3 Espire 2 update, which includes public matchmaking for our co-op campaign. We hope you enjoy matchmaking with another stealth operative around the globe to experience the co-op missions! We also recently added in-game voice chat functionality.

We also have released our public roadmap for 2023 and it includes new content coming soon! (for Single player and Co-Op). If you enjoy these updates, we'd appreciate if you'd consider updating your review, all the support helps! 

Cheers from the team at Lode!

developer_response.body: Hi there! Thanks for your positive review and support and we hope you continue to enjoy Espire 2! Cheers.

reviews.review_description:
Not as good as the first, but still fun and fast paced and stealthy. Also very active updates. I enjoy it very much

Update: so the developers asked me to update feedback (yes, they actually listen!). So I think that this game is lacking is a more complete story, and also I’m not super crazy about the weird vision update (when you move, even on normal, your vision is like a super small tunnel). But overall I think this was a great game and pretty cool. 4.9/5.

Update: changed my rating to 5/5 because of how AMAZING public matchmaking is. Thank you devs, it’s pretty awesome

I was looking for a stealth game when I just got into vr which has good physics and a lot of mechanics. This game has everything that I wanted from a stealth game as it's very fun and has really good graphics on the quest 3 and the mixed reality is also very fun. Only the AI soldiers are quite stupid however it's not something that ruins the fun.

reviews.review_title: I just wanna say love the game
reviews.review_description: This is one of the best games I’ve ever played the only thing I wish would be in the game is the fact that you can host the game or join a random persons game without a code

developer_response.body: Hey there! We are so grateful for your review! Re joining a random person's game, we've recently added a public match making feature - it will match you with another random player who wants to join your game. We're also working on improved match-making options to better select appropriate players - more to come on that front! Thanks for supporting Espire! Cheers.

reviews.review_title: This app is awesome minus initial issues
reviews.review_description: This app is awesome. At first I had issues launching the app after it installed on my new Quest 3. When I clicked start it would get to the initial splash page with the three dots at the bottom then would kick me back to the app gallery. After trying many times then uninstalling and reinstalling a few times, I was about to give up then tried one last time and it launched. Once I got passed that road lock, I must admit it was smooth sailing. The augmented reality section specifically is so good. My basement has never been so fun to hang out in solving missions. If it was not for the issues I had at first it would have been a 5  stars.

developer_response.body:
Hi there, we are sorry to hear you had issues installing the game. We actually reported your issue to Meta's engineering team. It is an issue with many VR games at the moment that are above 4GB in size. Meta has identified a fix and will include it in a future OS update.  We know how frustrating an issue it is, as the only workaround is to uninstall and reinstall the game. 

We are grateful that you persisted beyond this issue and glad to hear it was smooth sailing after that. If you have any further feedback, you can feel free to update your review, we will get a notification. We hope Espire 2 continues to entertain. Thanks from LODE, developers of Espire. .

reviews.review_description:
From what I’ve played so far, the game is amazing. The stealth is very realistic, the weapons system feels new, and the graphics are decent for a mobile VR game. The game does have very small performance issues, but nothing game breaking.

My favorite part of the game so far, is the voice commands. The voice commands are very precise, and it works every time. 

The performance issues i’m having is mostly a little lag here and there.

developer_response.body: Hi Ironman2000015, thanks for leaving us a review! If you're happy to take the time, could you update this review and let us know where you're experiencing those performance issues you mentioned? We're actively working on improving and updating the game over time, so we'd love to make a note for future bug patches. We're really pleased that you enjoy the voice commands, they're one of the dev team's favorite features! Thanks so much for playing :)

reviews.review_title: Top Game MR for me on Quest 3
reviews.review_description: After a difficult Setup at the beginning due to my small Room I follow the creator suggestion and it was really fun in Mixed Reality on Meta quest 3 I like this game well done 👍😀 the Laser in the Room it’s amazing for me seems to stay in a movie 🎥

developer_response.body: Hi there, thanks for taking the time to leave a review of your experience and we're so glad you've been having fun in our MR mode. We hope Espire 2 continues to entertain, with it's MR other VR game modes. Cheers from LODE - developers of Espire.

reviews.review_description: the starting position is always behind the virtual fence. so unable to start. i will give ig back.

developer_response.body:
Hi there, we have a workaround for this issue: Your 'Guardian boundary' is smaller than your 'Scanned Room'. To play our Mixed Reality game, you will need to draw a large 'roomscale' guardian boundary that is much bigger than your actual scanned room. 

We are sorry you've experienced this issue. At the time of writing, this is out of our control - both the guardian boundary + Scanned room must be enabled. We hope the Meta system OS is updated in the future so this is not required anymore. 

Hope this resolves your issue and if so, we would appreciate if you'd consider updating your review. Cheers from the team @ LODE.

reviews.review_title: I can't find how to save progress
reviews.review_description: Saving progress in a game is a pretty basic function and it's unimaginably frustrating when I Google it and can't find a single response which is odd to me never mind in the game itself I can't find anything that has to do with saving progress I'm hesitant to buy my daughter a game to also play with me until I can get past this why would they make it so hard to find

developer_response.body:
Hi there. We're sorry for any confusion here and hope we can help. The game saves automatically. When you're playing the long campaign missions, there are 3-4 checkpoints you'll pass through, and the game will say 'mission progress saved'. You can safely quit the Espire 2 app at that point and resume from the checkpoint. 

Every time you complete a mission or piece of content, the game will save. Every time you adjust your settings, the settings will be saved. 

We're sorry that this has been confusing. Let us know if there is anything else we can help you with, and if there's other feedback we can take onboard, would love to turn this into a 5 star review one day. Cheers from the team @ Lode.

reviews.review_title: If you like stealth games get it
reviews.review_description: I have not played too much but I love it but for the missions/levels I think I would be a great quality of life change if you could save at a part so if you want you don’t have to restart the hole level if you want to be not spotted like dishonored 2 ￼

developer_response.body: Hello LuigiTrap! We're so glad to hear you're loving Espire 2! A quick note on the saving: everytime you see a 'loading screen' - that's a save checkpoint. There are approx 3 checkpoints per mission. If you press 'abort mission', you can 'resume' the mission from the checkpoint. Hearing your feedback, i think we could make a 'restart from checkpoint' button. We've never thought of that. We hope in the meantime, the abort mission -> resume mission advice can help you!
